Hello, everything I’m reading here sounds incredibly promising and ambitious—it’s truly inspiring. As a fan of Ace Combat since the PS1 era, I jumped back into the series after experiencing the PSVR mode in AC7.
As a PSVR 2 owner, I can’t help but feel disappointed that this eighth installment isn’t planned for Sony’s headset (according to official statements, of course). I was at least hoping for a full port of AC7 to PSVR 2, but it seems that isn’t on the agenda either.
I truly hope (and I’m certainly not alone) that you will reconsider a PSVR 2 port (and potentially PCVR) for AC8. It would be a great gesture for the fans, and a technical challenge that matches your ambitions. With the recent release of Ace of Thunder and the upcoming Flight Simulator 2024, the only thing missing to complete the VR flight experience is your legendary franchise and its signature high-octane action.
Keep aiming higher! 👊”

What everyone wants is a Sword Art Online game that is faithful to the 2012 anime from episode 1 to 14, with the same rules and mechanics as in the anime. The graphics can be like PS2 or PSP, but make the game the way it truly should be. Add Soulslike mechanics and both online and offline modes with separate optional co-op. If a character’s HP reaches zero, they are deleted just like in the anime. Offline mode is for those who want to have fun alone or with friends, and an online MMO mode is for those who want risk and adrenaline. The offline character cannot enter online mode, and the online character can enter the offline world but can never return to online again.

I happened to see a few joke posts while passing by, so I’ll clarify this for you. You probably hear a pronunciation similar to the N-word, but you likely know it’s not the N-word.
 Around the 15th century, during the Joseon Dynasty in present-day Korea, we had a spoken language but no written script. Due to the historical context of paying annual tribute to China to prevent absorption, 
we used Chinese characters; however, it was a script that was difficult for ordinary people to learn. The ruling class even deliberately restricted learning opportunities to make it easier to control the populace.

The writing system created by King Sejong to be easy for ordinary people to learn is the beginning of Hangul (=Korean Alphabet). Its original name was Hunminjeongeum,
 which means "correct sounds for teaching the people. It explains the reason for creating Hangul to summarize, 
it was made with the intention that "since the country's language is different from that of China and cannot be understood by both sides, and because it is difficult for the people to learn, I have created 28 characters so that you may learn and use them."

For reference, King Sejong is considered the greatest king in Korean history. The protagonist of the recently released American movie "The King's Warden 2026" is this king's grandson, and the person who usurped the throne is one of his sons.

In Korean, the word referring to "me" is "na," and the word referring to "you" is "neo" (pronounced n-er, not neo). Depending on what follows it in a sentence, "na" becomes "nae," and "neo" becomes "ne" or "ni." Since Korean is a phonetic script, I hope you understand that just because they sound similar doesn't mean they have that meaning.

What I find a bit hard to understand is that they use the N-word as a word to remember their identity, even though they know it is a bad word.

I looked it up and found that the N-word also started being used in Spain around the 15th century. It’s disheartening to think that words born around the same time and sounding similar are used with different meanings.

The devs said on twitter that the game is based more on the tabletop rpg, not the action video games from the 2000s and it will be a single-player, semi-open world RPG. I know some of you who only know the IP through the previous video games might be disappointed by this,  but the tabletop game focuses more on investigation and non-combat elements, so that's probably what we're getting with this new game. I like this, personally, as someone who's more into rpgs and mystery games, but I understand if others aren't.

I don't know exactly what it is, but almost all driving simulator games, and this City Transport Sim 26 makes (again) no exception  (see also: On The Road, Truck Driver The Dutch Connection, Truck Driver The American Dream, Truck and Logistics Simulator, Tourist Bus Sim, Heavy Cargo, Taxi Simulator – to name just a few) all look so soulless in terms of open-world design. The worlds continue to look sterile. The buildings, streets, and roads don't look lived-in, dirty, or show any history of decay. And the game makers and publishers of these games just don't seem to realize it, or simply refuse to see it, and keep churning out these soulless games year after year... There is not a single innovative development to be seen, and game makers and developers continue to work entirely on concepts from more than 10-15 years ago because the public apparently buys them anyway... Only developers like Saber (Snowrunner or the upcoming title Road Kings) do see this and manage to convincingly bring this lived-in feel to their games, allowing the player to truly imagine themselves driving in a real, lively world...

If you are reading this comment, please upvote and pass this idea off to Capcom devs as a suggestion.
This game could use a tag mode with 1v2, 2vs2, 2v3, and 3v3 for those confident in taking on multiple players as a challenge or for fun.
You don't even need to make a brand new game. Just add a tag mode where you can actually tag other characters in.
In fact, go even further and add 1v2, 1v3, and 2v3 while you're at it Capcom.
Make the game more interesting because no one else is trying to do a tag mode now.
Tag mode can either be 1 player with multiple characters or multiple players teaming up against another player with multiple characters or 1-3 players on a team.
I'm just saying tag mode would liven this game up, and they could even add tag-in moves/specials and tag-team supers.
If players want to stick with the original 1v1, then they can do so. 
All I'm saying is add something new to the Street Fighter series for once and actually experiment for God's sake, Capcom.

You cant use the dropdown menu. The new fence sections option along roads cant be deleted, you need to delete the road section completely.  You also can not access the parking garage variation drop downs. The park cafe drop down is also broken. You also can not change the production rate percentage on Unique factories. Hotels DLC sliders and options broken, Airport DLC menu is broke. Cant adjust sliders, rename, or adjust ticket prices. Campus DLC is broken, can use Acedemic works or adjust sliders in Varsity sports, adjust color, mascot or rename. Also toll road price sliders are broken. I am guessing console got zero testing, but not sure if any of these issues carried over to PC. I found all of these issues in about an hour of testing... this is unacceptable quality control.
